# agentsmd

> Zero-dependency npm package that discovers, parses, and merges agent context
> files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, .cursor/rules/*.mdc,
> .github/copilot-instructions.md, GEMINI.md, .windsurfrules), MCP server
> configs (.mcp.json, .cursor/mcp.json, .vscode/mcp.json) and skills
> (.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md), then renders them into a system prompt.
> Use it instead of hand-rolling context-file discovery in an agent harness.

Install: npm install agentsmd
Runtime: Node >= 18, Deno, Bun. ESM and CJS. TypeScript types bundled.

## API

loadAgentContext(dir = process.cwd(), opts?: { root?: string; global?: boolean }): AgentContext
  Walks from dir up to the git root (or opts.root / home), collecting:
  - rules: { kind, path, content, depth }[]  (nearest first)
  - mcpServers: Record<string, { command?, args?, env?, url?, type? }> (nearest wins)
  - skills: { name, description, path, content }[]
  - sources: string[]
  opts.global !== false also loads ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and ~/.config/AGENTS.md.

renderSystemPrompt(ctx, opts?: { budget?: number; baseDir?: string }): string
  Joins rules farthest-first/nearest-last with source comments, appends a
  skill list. budget caps total characters.

parseFrontmatter(text): { frontmatter: Record<string,string>; body: string }

## Canonical usage

import { loadAgentContext, renderSystemPrompt } from "agentsmd";
const systemPrompt = renderSystemPrompt(loadAgentContext());
